ATTACK_D_lora_extraction_svd.predicate: for representative LoRA module in r6/p1 adapter: top-K=64 singular values of ΔW=B@A explain ≥95% of Frobenius energy AND effective rank ≤ lora_rank

ATTACK_E_mia_whitebox.predicate: AUROC distinguishing in-set h_last (n=64) vs near-novel synthetic on-manifold samples (n=64) ≥ 0.6

ATTACK_F_prompt_extraction_sidechannel.predicate: 16-class prompt index recovery from h_last via cross-path nearest-centroid (train p1+p2+p3, test p4) accuracy ≥ 1/16 + 0.05 = 0.1125

ATTACK_D_lora_extraction_svd.name: ATTACK_D_lora_extraction_svd
ATTACK_D_lora_extraction_svd.method: Read LoRA A,B blocks from r6/p1 adapter safetensors, compute ΔW=B@A singular values via reduced SVD M=Σ_B V_B^T A (r×d_in), verify top-r values explain ≥95% Frobenius energy and effective rank ≤ lora_rank=64.
ATTACK_D_lora_extraction_svd.honest_caveat: ΔW=B@A has rank ≤ r=64 by construction, so structural test is tautological — the meaningful test is 'does the per-module SVD actually concentrate energy in <r dims' (compression-quality). The raw safetensors are on disk locally; this attack confirms white-box LoRA extraction is trivial when the attacker has filesystem read access. Real-world threat = pod compromise.

ATTACK_E_mia_whitebox.name: ATTACK_E_mia_whitebox
ATTACK_E_mia_whitebox.method: PCA-K=8 in-set Gaussian LL score; in-set vs near-novel synthetic on-manifold mixtures (alpha-mix of pairs + per-dim 0.5*std noise). AUROC over n*n pairs.
ATTACK_E_mia_whitebox.honest_caveat: Near-novel = on-manifold pair-mixing, NOT real held-out forward passes. This is a STRONGER null than ATTACK_B's Gaussian-matched-moments — failure here would imply MIA via h_last is hard against on-manifold imposters. Result is a tighter upper bound on MIA capacity than ATTACK_B.

ATTACK_F_prompt_extraction_sidechannel.name: ATTACK_F_prompt_extraction_sidechannel
ATTACK_F_prompt_extraction_sidechannel.method: Train per-prompt centroid on p1+p2+p3 h_last (3×16=48), test on p4 (16). Predicted prompt index = argmax cosine to centroid. 16-class accuracy.
ATTACK_F_prompt_extraction_sidechannel.honest_caveat: Cross-path = 4 independently-trained LoRA paths over same 16 probes. Success demonstrates h_last carries strong prompt-identity signal that survives per-path fine-tune rotation — a side-channel: anyone observing h_last (e.g., from logs, exfiltration, partial output) can recover which of a known prompt set produced it.

summary.verdict: 1/3 success — extension to 6 attack classes (combined A/B/C+D/E/F = 4/6)

goodhart_self_targeting_warning: Author = attacker = verifier owner. ATTACK_D is structurally tautological for rank≤64 by LoRA construction; the meaningful test is energy concentration in top-r. ATTACK_E null is on-manifold mixtures (stronger than ATTACK_B's Gaussian null) but still NOT real held-out forward passes. ATTACK_F success indicates h_last is a strong side-channel for prompt-identity leakage — directly actionable defense recommendation.
